message:
refactor(fwc): make rate_limits and archetypes Optional to distinguish absent from empty

Previously, connector and operation rate_limits and archetypes were
represented as Vec<_>, making it impossible to distinguish "this source
confirms there are zero rate limits" from "this source cannot surface
trustworthy rate-limit metadata at all." The same ambiguity applied
to connector archetypes.

Type changes:
- ConnectorSummary::archetypes: Vec<String> → Option<Vec<String>>
- ConnectorDetail::rate_limits: Vec<RateLimitSummary> → Option<Vec<…>>
- DiscoveredOperation::rate_limits: Vec<RateLimitSummary> → Option<Vec<…>>
- PipelineOperationMetadata::rate_limits: Vec<…> → Option<Vec<…>>
- PipelineStepEstimate::rate_limits: Vec<…> → Option<Vec<…>>

Behavioral changes:
- Pipeline estimation now tracks steps_with_unknown_rate_limits separately
  from steps_without_declared_limits, emitting a distinct note when a
  step's source did not surface trustworthy metadata (None) vs. confirmed
  the connector has no limits (Some(empty vec))
- TOML-based fallback connector discovery sets rate_limits to None
  instead of empty vec, since raw TOML cannot prove structured rate-limit
  declarations
- All .iter().flatten() patterns added for archetypes to safely traverse
  the Option wrapper in search filters and category matching

New helpers in main.rs:
- human_window_duration(): formats Duration as human-readable "1s"/"60s"/"1h"/"1d"
- host_rate_limit_summaries(): deduplicates and formats pool-level limits
- host_connector_archetypes(): extracts archetype labels from host introspection
- host_connector_rate_limits(): extracts connector-level limits from host
- host_operation_rate_limits(): merges pool-map and inline tool limits

catalog.rs: rustfmt formatting applied to long assertion chains and
map_or closures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -237,6 +237,7 @@ fn connector_passes_filters(connector: &DiscoveredConnector, filters: &SearchFil
             .summary
             .archetypes
             .iter()
+            .flatten()
             .any(|a| a.to_lowercase() == arch_lower)
         {
             return false;
@@ -412,7 +413,7 @@ mod tests {
                     name: format!("{} Connector", capitalize(slug)),
                     version: "0.1.0".to_owned(),
                     description: format!("FCP connector for {slug}"),
-                    archetypes: vec!["operational".to_owned()],
+                    archetypes: Some(vec!["operational".to_owned()]),
                     state: ConnectorState::Unknown,
                     operation_count: ops.len(),
                     max_risk: "medium".to_owned(),
@@ -421,7 +422,7 @@ mod tests {
                 operations: op_summaries,
                 config_schema: None,
                 health: None,
-                rate_limits: vec![],
+                rate_limits: Some(vec![]),
             },
             zones: json!({}),
             capabilities: json!({}),
@@ -462,7 +463,7 @@ mod tests {
             examples: vec![],
             related: vec![],
             network_constraints: None,
-            rate_limits: vec![],
+            rate_limits: Some(vec![]),
         }
     }
